content:
# John Chapter 18 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_018_page_001.jpg, JHN_018_page_002.jpg

1. Having said these things, the Yeshua went out with the disciples of him beyond the torrent of the Kedron, where there was a garden, into which he entered, he and the disciples of him.

2. And Ioudas also, the [one] betraying him, knew the place, because often the Yeshua gathered there with the disciples of him.

3. Then Ioudas, having taken the cohort and attendants from the chief priests and the Pharisaioi, comes there with lanterns and torches and weapons.

4. Then the Yeshua, knowing all the things coming upon him, went out and says to them, "Whom do you seek?"

5. They answered him, "Yeshua the Nazoraios." He says to them, "I am." And Ioudas also, the [one] betraying him, was standing with them.

6. When therefore he said to them, "I am," they went backward and fell to the ground.

7. Again therefore he asked them, "Whom do you seek?" And they said, "Yeshua the Nazoraios."

8. The Yeshua answered, "I told you that I am. If therefore me you seek, let these go,"

9. that the word might be fulfilled that he said, "Of those whom you have given me, I did not lose one."

10. Then Simon Petros, having a sword, drew it and struck the slave of the high priest and cut off the ear of him, the right [one]. And the name of the slave was Malchos.

11. Then the Yeshua said to Petros, "Put the sword into the sheath. The cup that the Father has given me, shall I not drink it?"

12. Then the cohort and the tribune and the attendants of the Ioudaioi seized the Yeshua and bound him,

13. and led [him] to Annas first; for he was father-in-law of Kaiaphas, who was high priest of that year.

14. And Kaiaphas was the [one] having counseled the Ioudaioi that it is better [for] one man to die for the people.

15. And Simon Petros was following the Yeshua, and another disciple. And that disciple was known to the high priest and entered with the Yeshua into the court of the high priest;

16. but Petros was standing at the door outside. Then the other disciple, the [one] known to the high priest, went out and spoke to the doorkeeper and brought Petros in.

17. Then the servant girl, the doorkeeper, says to Petros, "Are not you also [one] of the disciples of this man?" That [one] says, "I am not."

18. And the slaves and the attendants were standing, having made a charcoal fire because it was cold, and they were warming themselves; and Petros also was with them, standing and warming himself.

19. Then the high priest asked the Yeshua concerning the disciples of him and concerning the teaching of him.

20. The Yeshua answered him, "I spoke openly to the world. I always taught in [the] synagogue and in the temple, where all the Ioudaioi gather, and in secret I spoke nothing.

21. Why do you ask me? Ask the [ones] who have heard what I spoke to them. Behold, these know what I said."

22. And when he said these things, one of the attendants standing by gave the Yeshua a slap, saying, "Thus you answer the high priest?"

23. The Yeshua answered him, "If I spoke wrongly, testify concerning the wrong; but if rightly, why do you strike me?"

24. Then Annas sent him bound to Kaiaphas the high priest.

25. And Simon Petros was standing and warming himself. Then they said to him, "Are not you also [one] of the disciples of him?" That [one] denied and said, "I am not."

26. One of the slaves of the high priest, being a relative of the [one] whose ear Petros cut off, says, "Did I not see you in the garden with him?"

27. Then again Petros denied, and immediately a rooster crowed.

28. Then they lead the Yeshua from Kaiaphas to the Praetorium. And it was early. And they did not enter into the Praetorium, that they might not be defiled but might eat the Passover.

29. Then Pilatos went out to them and says, "What accusation do you bring against this man?"

30. They answered and said to him, "If this [one] were not doing evil, we would not have handed him over to you."

31. Then Pilatos said to them, "Take him yourselves and according to the law of you judge him." The Ioudaioi said to him, "It is not permitted for us to kill anyone,"

32. that the word of the Yeshua might be fulfilled that he said signifying by what kind of death he was about to die.

33. Then Pilatos entered into the Praetorium again and called the Yeshua and said to him, "You are the king of the Ioudaioi?"

34. The Yeshua answered, "From yourself do you say this, or did others tell you concerning me?"

35. Pilatos answered, "Am I a Ioudaios? The nation of you and the chief priests handed you over to me. What did you do?"

36. The Yeshua answered, "The kingdom of me is not of this world. If from this world were the kingdom of me, the attendants of me would have fought, that I might not be handed over to the Ioudaioi. But now the kingdom of me is not from here."

37. Then Pilatos said to him, "Then you are a king?" The Yeshua answered, "You say that I am a king. For this I have been born and for this I have come into the world, that I may testify to the truth. Everyone being of the truth hears the voice of me."

38. Pilatos says to him, "What is truth?" And having said this, again he went out to the Ioudaioi and says to them, "I find no guilt in him.

39. But there is a custom for you that I release one [person] to you at the Passover. Therefore do you wish [that] I release to you the king of the Ioudaioi?"

40. Then they cried out again, saying, "Not this [one], but Barabbas!" And Barabbas was a robber.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "The Kedron":** τοῦ Κεδρών — the Kidron Valley, east of Jerusalem between the city and the Mount of Olives. David crossed the Kidron fleeing Absalom's rebellion (2 Samuel 15:23). Jesus crosses it going to his arrest.

2. **v.5-6 — "I am" (ἐγώ εἰμι):** When Jesus says "I am," the soldiers fall backward to the ground. This is the divine name in action — the same words that Moses heard at the burning bush. Even in his arrest, Jesus demonstrates sovereign power. He is not captured; he surrenders.

3. **v.10 — "Malchos":** Only John names the servant (Malchos) and specifies the right ear. The detail suggests eyewitness testimony, likely from the "other disciple" (v.15) who was known to the high priest.

4. **v.28 — Passover chronology:** John's chronology differs from the Synoptics. In John, Jesus is crucified as the Passover lambs are being slaughtered (19:14). The Jewish leaders have not yet eaten the Passover (18:28). Jesus IS the Passover lamb — John's timing is theological.

5. **v.38 — "What is truth?":** Pilate's question has no answer recorded — he walks away before Jesus can respond. The irony is supreme: Pilate asks "What is truth?" while standing before the one who said "I am the truth" (14:6). The question hangs unanswered in the text, but John's readers know the answer.

— EMET, AI Translator
